concepts.name: Cell Structure & Function (Prokaryotes and Eukaryotes)
concepts.description: Introduction to biology via the concept of cell — difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; sub-cellular organelles (nucleus, mitochondria, ribosome, membrane, cytoplasm), cell membrane structure, basic functions of each organelle.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Visualize a cell in 2D/3D, toggle organelles, see structure and labels; allow user to compare prokaryotic vs eukaryotic cells; highlight organelle functions.

learning_objectives: Identify major cell types (prokaryotic vs eukaryotic).
Recognize and name key organelles and their functions.
Explain the role of the cell membrane and cytoplasm.

concepts.name: Basic Genetics: DNA, Genes, and Heredity (intro)
concepts.description: Introduction to DNA as genetic material, structure of DNA (double helix, nucleotides), concept of genes, chromosomes, inheritance basics, basic idea of dominant and recessive traits, simple Mendelian inheritance.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Visualize DNA double helix, show gene on chromosome; allow “cross-pollination” of alleles and simulate simple Mendelian inheritance across generations to observe trait inheritance.

learning_objectives: Describe DNA structure and its role as genetic material.
Define genes, alleles, chromosomes and basic heredity terms.
Simulate simple inheritance of traits using Mendelian model.

concepts.name: Photosynthesis & Cellular Respiration (intro)
concepts.description: Basic processes of photosynthesis (in plants) and cellular respiration (in all cells) — understand energy capture, conversion, usage; basic overall chemical equations; role of chloroplasts / mitochondria.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Visualize plant cell / leaf chloroplasts doing photosynthesis, and animal cell mitochondria doing respiration; animate exchange of gases and energy flow; show energy output/consumption.

cross_domain_links.notes: Chemical understanding of molecules and energy helps in understanding reaction basics

learning_objectives: Explain basic steps and purpose of photosynthesis and respiration.
Identify organelles involved (chloroplast, mitochondria).
Recognize the role of these processes in energy flow for organisms.

concepts.name: Ecology & Ecosystems (intro to living systems)
concepts.description: Introduction to ecological concepts: organism, population, community, ecosystem; energy flow, producers/consumers/decomposers, food chains/webs; basic ecosystem balance and human impact idea.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Visualize a simple ecosystem (plants, herbivores, carnivores, decomposers), animate energy flow and matter cycling, simulate perturbations (e.g. species removal) to show ecosystem balance effects.

learning_objectives: Define organism, population, community, ecosystem.
Explain energy flow and trophic levels (producers, consumers, decomposers).
Illustrate how ecosystem balance can be affected by changes in species or environment.

concepts.name: Basic Human Anatomy & Physiology (cells → organs) — introduction
concepts.description: Introduce idea that multicellular organisms have specialized organs & tissues — overview of major tissue types (epithelial, muscle, nerve, connective) and major organ systems (digestive, circulatory, respiratory) at a high level.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Show simplified human body schematic, allow toggling organ systems and tissues; show basic flow (e.g. blood circulation, respiration, digestion flow), to build structural understanding.

learning_objectives: Recognize major tissue types and their roles.
Identify major organ systems and their basic function.
Understand that body functions rely on coordinated organ/tissue systems.

concepts.name: Cell Division, Mitosis & Meiosis
concepts.description: Understand how cells reproduce — details of mitosis and meiosis, cell cycle phases, chromosome duplication/separation, genetic variation via meiosis, basics of gametes vs somatic cells.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Animate cell division cycles, visualize chromosome duplication/separation, simulate meiosis with crossing over and show resulting variation in gametes.

learning_objectives: Describe steps of mitosis and meiosis and distinguish between them.
Understand chromosome duplication and segregation.
Explain how meiosis introduces genetic variation.

concepts.name: Molecular Biology: Transcription, Translation & Gene Expression
concepts.description: Explain central dogma: DNA → RNA → Protein; processes of transcription and translation, role of mRNA, tRNA, ribosomes; basics of gene regulation (on/off), basic mutations and effect on protein function.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Animate transcription and translation processes in a cell; allow user to modify DNA sequence and observe how mutations affect mRNA and resulting amino-acid chain, visualize protein synthesis.

learning_objectives: Describe transcription and translation steps in detail.
Understand how genes encode proteins and how mutations can affect protein sequences.
Relate gene expression changes to potential phenotype changes.

concepts.name: Evolution & Natural Selection (intro to evolutionary biology)
concepts.description: Basics of evolution: variation, heritability, natural selection; population genetics ideas; adaptation; speciation concept; basic ecological and evolutionary pressures.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate a population over generations under selective pressures; show trait distribution change over time; allow user to vary mutation rate, selection pressure, environment to observe adaptation and possible speciation.

learning_objectives: Explain principles of natural selection, variation, heritability.
Model how populations evolve over time under selective pressures.
Understand how adaptation and speciation can occur through evolutionary mechanisms.

concepts.name: Physiology & Homeostasis (organ systems regulation)
concepts.description: Deeper look at organ systems (nervous, circulatory, respiratory, endocrine), how they maintain homeostasis, feedback loops, basics of regulation (e.g. hormonal, neural), overview of system interactions.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate interacting organ-systems; model how changes (e.g. oxygen intake, hormone levels, stress) affect homeostasis; visualize feedback loops and regulation over time.

learning_objectives: Explain how organ systems work together to maintain homeostasis.
Model feedback loops and regulatory mechanisms in physiology.
Understand effects of perturbations (e.g. stress, environment change) on body regulation.

concepts.name: Molecular & Cellular Signaling Pathways
concepts.description: Study of how cells communicate: receptor-ligand binding, signal transduction cascades, second messengers, gene expression regulation via signaling, cell-to-cell communication, feedback regulation in signaling pathways.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to build signaling pathways (receptors, ligands, cascades), simulate activation/inhibition, propagate signals, observe downstream effects (gene expression, cellular responses).

learning_objectives: Construct and interpret cellular signaling pathways.
Simulate how signals propagate and regulate cell behavior or gene expression.
Understand feedback and regulation in cellular communication.

concepts.name: Population Genetics, Evolutionary Dynamics & Speciation Modeling
concepts.description: Quantitative and simulation-based analysis of population genetics: allele frequency dynamics, genetic drift, selection, mutation, migration; modeling speciation, population bottlenecks, Hardy–Weinberg equilibrium, genetic variation over time under different pressures.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to simulate populations over many generations, set parameters (selection coefficients, mutation rates, population size, migration), track allele frequencies, observe effects like drift, fixation, speciation over time.

learning_objectives: Model allele-frequency changes over time under different evolutionary forces.
Understand and apply population-genetics concepts (drift, selection, mutation, migration, bottleneck).
Simulate potential speciation events and analyze evolutionary dynamics quantitatively.

concepts.name: Developmental Biology & Tissue Differentiation
concepts.description: Study of how multicellular organisms develop: cell differentiation, developmental stages, stem cells, gene regulation during development, tissue formation, pattern formation, morphogenesis, regulatory networks guiding development.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate cell lineage differentiation, tissue formation over developmental time, allow toggling gene-regulation rules to see effects on differentiation, visualize morphology and pattern development.

learning_objectives: Understand how cells differentiate and form tissues and organs.
Model developmental processes including gene regulation, lineage decisions and morphogenesis.
Visualize how developmental rules affect final organism structure and function.

concepts.name: Immunology & Host-Pathogen Interactions (intro to immune system)
concepts.description: Study of basic immune system: innate immunity, adaptive immunity, cells involved (white blood cells, lymphocytes), antibodies, antigens, immune response cascade; basics of infection, immunity, vaccination, and host-pathogen dynamics.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate infection and immune response dynamics — introduce pathogen, simulate immune cell responses, antibody production, pathogen clearance, immune memory; allow varying pathogen virulence or immune strength to observe outcomes.

learning_objectives: Describe key components and pathways of innate and adaptive immunity.
Simulate immune response to pathogen invasion and understand dynamics of infection and clearance.
Understand principles of immunity, vaccination, immune memory, and host-pathogen interactions.

concepts.name: Systems Biology & Multi-Scale Modeling (cell → tissue → organism)
concepts.description: Use computational modeling to simulate biological systems across scales: gene regulatory networks, cell signaling networks, tissue systems, organ systems, whole-organism behavior; integrate molecular, cellular, physiological data to model complex behavior and emergent properties.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to define networks at multiple scales (molecular, cellular, tissue), simulate dynamic behavior over time, visualize emergent outcomes, perturb system (e.g. gene knock-out, environmental change) and observe systemic effects.

learning_objectives: Construct and simulate biological systems at multiple scales (gene, cell, tissue, organ).
Analyze emergent behavior from complex interactions.
Use simulations to test hypotheses, predict outcomes of perturbations (genetic, environmental).

concepts.name: Evolutionary Genomics & Computational Phylogenetics
concepts.description: Use large-scale genomic data, bioinformatics and computational methods to study evolution — comparative genomics, phylogenetic tree construction, molecular evolution, gene family evolution, population genomics analyses, speciation events, genomic adaptation to environment.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to load or generate genomic sequence data, perform alignment, construct phylogenetic trees, compute evolutionary distances, simulate speciation or divergence under different evolutionary models, visualize genomic variation across populations or species.

learning_objectives: Perform sequence alignment and comparative genomics analyses.
Construct and interpret phylogenetic trees.
Model evolutionary divergence and speciation computationally.
Analyze genome-level adaptation and variation across species/populations.

concepts.name: Epidemiology & Population Health Modeling
concepts.description: Use computational models to simulate disease spread, host population interactions, epidemiological dynamics; model infectious disease outbreaks, herd immunity, intervention strategies; integrate biology, environment, population behavior to study public-health phenomena.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to define population parameters (susceptible, infected, recovered), transmission rates, interventions (vaccination, isolation), simulate outbreak over time, visualize curves, explore impact of parameters and control strategies.

learning_objectives: Construct epidemiological models (SIR, SEIR etc.) and simulate disease spread.
Analyze effects of interventions (vaccination, isolation, social measures) on outbreak dynamics.
Understand population-level health dynamics and factors influencing disease propagation.
